data.title: GREEN: Update sprint-next-batch.sh to handle unlimited and 0 --limit values
data.description:
## Description

Update sprint-next-batch.sh to handle the new --limit semantics: unlimited (no cap) and 0 (empty batch).

### Files to modify
- plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-next-batch.sh

### Implementation details
1. Change --limit=0 semantic: currently 0 means unlimited; change to 0 means empty batch (return BATCH_SIZE: 0 immediately)
2. Add --limit=unlimited sentinel: treat as no cap (same as omitting --limit)
3. When --limit is omitted: default to unlimited (no cap) — preserves current behavior for callers that do not pass --limit
4. Update usage comment at top of file to document new semantics

### Backward compatibility
- Callers that passed --limit=0 for unlimited must be updated (but these are in skill prompts, not scripts)
- Callers that passed positive integers are unaffected

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-next-batch.sh (modify)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All RED tests from Task 3 now pass (GREEN)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh
- [ ] --limit=0 returns BATCH_SIZE: 0
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh
- [ ] --limit=unlimited returns full pool
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-next-batch-limit.sh
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
